pattern: Find every triangle, then its outward degree is the sum of the three nodes' degrees minus the six internal endpoints.
hints: A connected trio is just a triangle in the graph, so first you need a way to detect triangles.
For a triangle on nodes a, b, c, the outward degree counts edges leaving the trio, which is deg(a)+deg(b)+deg(c) minus the six endpoints used by the three internal edges.
Enumerate edges and for each pair of endpoints check shared neighbors (using an adjacency matrix); track the minimum outward degree, returning -1 if no triangle exists.

edge_inputs.description: no triangle at all returns minus one
two nodes cannot form a trio
complete graph on four nodes, all trios have degree two

stress.description: random graph on a modest node count, guaranteed to contain at least one triangle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 80))
    edge_set = set()
    edge_set.add((1, 2)); edge_set.add((1, 3)); edge_set.add((2, 3))
    extra = rng.randrange(n, 3 * n)
    for _ in range(extra):
        u = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        v = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        if u == v:
            continue
        a, b = min(u, v), max(u, v)
        edge_set.add((a, b))
    edges = [[a, b] for (a, b) in edge_set]
    return [n, edges]
